meta.title: Refresh auth tokens before expiry instead of on 401
meta.body: Our API client currently only refreshes the access token *after* a request fails with 401, which causes a user-visible retry latency spike and, under concurrency, a thundering-herd of refresh calls. This PR proactively refreshes the token when it is within 60s of expiry, guards concurrent refreshes with a single in-flight promise, and adds coverage for the race. Docs updated with the new env var.
meta.author: dana-eng

hunks.patch:
@@ -40,2 +40,5 @@ ## Configuration
 | Variable | Purpose |
 |---|---|
+| `ARGUS_TOKEN_SKEW_MS` | How early (ms) before expiry to refresh the access token. Default `60000`. |

review.summary: Switches the auth client from reactive (refresh-after-401) to proactive token refresh: it renews the access token when it is within a configurable skew window of expiry and coalesces concurrent refreshes behind a single in-flight promise. Adds a regression test for the concurrency case and documents the new skew env var.
review.intent: The author wants to remove the latency spike and refresh stampede caused by only refreshing on 401. The diff delivers this: getToken() now checks nearExpiry() and routes through refreshOnce(), which shares one Promise. Gap to verify: nothing here changes the actual 401 retry path, so if a token is revoked server-side early the old failure mode may still exist — confirm that is intentional and still handled elsewhere.
critical: refreshOnce() caches the in-flight promise but clears it in .finally(); confirm a rejected refresh does not get memoized and that the next getToken() retries rather than returning a rejected promise from a stale field.

nearExpiry() reads process.env on every call and coerces with Number(); a malformed ARGUS_TOKEN_SKEW_MS yields NaN, making the comparison always false and effectively disabling proactive refresh — needs validation or a fallback.
No test covers the near-expiry (non-zero skew) branch or a refresh() rejection; the added test only exercises the coalescing happy path.
flow: Start at getToken() in src/auth/token.ts: the entry point callers already use.
Follow the new nearExpiry() check that decides whether a refresh is needed.
Step into refreshOnce() to see how the single in-flight promise is created and cleared.
Read the added token.test.ts to see the intended concurrency contract.
Check README.md for the operator-facing knob that tunes the skew window.

files.note: Introduces proactive, coalesced token refresh via nearExpiry() and refreshOnce().
New regression test asserting concurrent getToken() calls trigger exactly one refresh.

hunks.why: Replaces the inline expiry check with a coalesced refresh path so simultaneous callers share one refresh instead of each firing their own.
hunks.lookout: The .finally() clears refreshInFlight on both success and failure — verify a rejected refresh isn't observed by a second awaiter as a shared rejection, and that clearing on reject can't race with a new caller assigning a fresh promise.

hunks.why: Adds a configurable skew so refresh happens before, not at, expiry.
hunks.lookout: Number(process.env...) returns NaN for garbage input, which silently disables proactive refresh; no lower/upper bound on skew either.

hunks.why: Locks in the coalescing contract with a concurrency test.
hunks.lookout: Only covers the happy path with expiresAt=0; does not test rejection cleanup or the real near-expiry window.
